nodes.label: exp_context
nodes.content: Photoelectrochemical cells constructed with CH3NH3PbX3 (X=Br, I) nanocrystalline particles self-organized on mesoporous TiO2 (8-12 um thick) deposited on FTO glass, using organic electrolyte with lithium halide/halogen redox couple, measured under AM 1.5 simulated sunlight at 100 mW/cm2

nodes.label: perovskite_materials
nodes.content: Organolead halide perovskite compounds CH3NH3PbBr3 (cubic, a=5.9 A) and CH3NH3PbI3 (tetragonal, a=8.855 A, c=12.659 A) synthesized from abundant sources Pb, C, N, and halogen, deposited from precursor solutions via spin-coating

nodes.label: pce_3_8_pct
nodes.content: CH3NH3PbI3-sensitized photoelectrochemical cell achieves power conversion efficiency of 3.81% under AM 1.5 simulated sunlight at 100 mW/cm2, with Jsc=11.0 mA/cm2, Voc=0.61 V, and fill factor 0.57

nodes.label: high_voc_bromide
nodes.content: CH3NH3PbBr3-sensitized cell yields a high open-circuit voltage of 0.96 V with Jsc=5.57 mA/cm2, fill factor 0.59, and PCE of 3.13%

nodes.label: perovskite_sensitizes_tio2
nodes.content: Organolead halide perovskite nanocrystalline particles efficiently sensitize mesoporous TiO2 for visible-light conversion, confirmed by anodic photocurrent generation and IPCE measurements

nodes.label: ipce_bromide_65_pct
nodes.content: CH3NH3PbBr3/TiO2 cell achieves maximum IPCE of 65% in the visible region (lambda < 600 nm) with sharp band-gap absorption onset near 570 nm

nodes.label: ipce_iodide_extended
nodes.content: CH3NH3PbI3/TiO2 cell shows extended spectral response to 800 nm but lower peak IPCE of 45%, reflecting broader but less intense absorption

nodes.label: bandgap_bathochromic_shift
nodes.content: Halogen substitution from Br to I in CH3NH3PbX3 causes a bathochromic shift in absorption, analogous to silver halide ionic crystals

nodes.label: valence_band_levels
nodes.content: Valence-band levels of CH3NH3PbBr3 and CH3NH3PbI3 are at ~5.38 and 5.44 eV versus vacuum level, respectively, as measured by photoelectron spectroscopy

nodes.label: conduction_band_levels
nodes.content: Conduction-band levels of CH3NH3PbBr3 and CH3NH3PbI3 are at ~3.36 and 4.0 eV respectively, allowing electron injection into the TiO2 conduction band (~4.0 eV)

nodes.label: high_voc_origin
nodes.content: The high Voc of 0.96 V in CH3NH3PbBr3 cells is due to the more positive electrochemical potential of the Br2/Br- redox couple compared to I2/I-, expanding the range of photovoltage

nodes.label: exceeds_quantum_dots
nodes.content: The 3.81% PCE of the CH3NH3PbI3 cell is significantly higher than efficiencies obtained to date with non-organic sensitizers and quantum dots (CdS, CdSe, PbS, InP, InAs)

nodes.label: perovskite_series_potential
nodes.content: A series of organic-inorganic perovskite materials CH3NH3MX3 (M=Pb, Sn; X=halogen) with different energy gaps are targets for optimizing photovoltaic cell performance

nodes.label: durability_question
nodes.content: How can the photocurrent decay observed under continuous irradiation for open cells exposed to air be mitigated to improve perovskite photovoltaic cell lifetime?

nodes.label: p_viability
nodes.title: p_viability
nodes.content: Organometal halide perovskites can function as viable photovoltaic absorbers

nodes.label: p_improvement
nodes.title: p_improvement
nodes.content: Perovskite solar cell efficiency can be continuously improved through materials engineering, interface engineering, and compositional engineering

nodes.label: p_stability
nodes.title: p_stability
nodes.content: Perovskite solar cells can achieve operational stability sufficient for practical deployment (>1000 hours under standard test conditions)

nodes.label: p_industrialization
nodes.title: p_industrialization
nodes.content: Perovskite solar cells can be scaled to module/panel level and industrially manufactured via roll-to-roll or slot-die processes
